name: SO-101
short: A fully 3D-printed robotic arm, assembled and programmed from scratch.
description: The SO-101 is an open-source robotic arm designed for accessibility and education. Fully 3D-printed with off-the-shelf servos, it is capable of teleoperation and autonomous pick-and-place tasks. Built as a personal project to explore low-cost manipulator design and control.

name: Line Follower Robot
short: An Arduino-based line-following robot built as a workshop series.
description: A line-following robot built using Arduino, IR sensors, and a motor driver. This was the focus of our workshop series where members learned the basics of electronics, sensor integration, and control logic. The project is based on an open-source Arduino design and adapted for our workshops.

name: Open Source wheeled humanoid (soon)
short: A project we are contributing to. More info coming soon.
